name: HMAS Adele (FY 089)
events: Franklin is remembered in the naming of two other sail training yachts associated with the RAN College a yacht in the 1960s and 1970s, which took part in several Sydney to Hobart races, and a 25 meter Hunter yacht acquired in 2005. (1)

14 Sep 1915 On 14 September 1915 the Yacht Adele was commissioned into the RAN as HMAS Franklin, named after Rear Admiral Sir John Franklin. She served as a tender to the Royal Australian Naval College. (1)
18 Sep 1922 Franklin was decommissioned on 18 September 1922 and gifted to the Administrator of Papua-New Guinea, serving there until 1929. (1)

24 Oct 1939 On 24 October 1939, Adele was requisitioned by the Royal Australian Navy as HMAS Adele. She was armed with two machine guns and employed as an examination vessel. From December 1941, she was attached to HMAS Maitland. (1)
7 May 1943 On 7 May 1943, Adele struck the breakwater at Port Kembla and was subsequently declared a total loss. The wreck of Adele is protected under the New South Wales Heritage Act, 1977. (1)
